attributes.description: Alerts when average disk queue depth is sustained above a threshold. High queue depth with plateauing IOPS is the canonical storage I/O saturation signature.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Disk queue depth high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Disk Queue Depth High

### Triage
1. Compare `ReadIOPS` and `WriteIOPS` to provisioned IOPS — plateauing at the ceiling confirms saturation.
2. Check `ReadLatency`/`WriteLatency` for rising storage latency.
3. On gp2, inspect `BurstBalance` for depleted burst credits.
4. Review recent workload changes that increased I/O demand.

### Mitigation
- Increase provisioned IOPS or upgrade storage type.
- Distribute read load to replicas.
- Optimize queries to reduce I/O (indexes, smaller scans).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND attributes.MetricName == "DiskQueueDepth"
    AND attributes.stat == "Average"
| STATS avg_disk_queue_depth = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DiskQueueDepth`)
    BY attributes.DBInstanceIdentifier, resource.attributes.cloud.region
// Queued I/O count — adjust based on workload (default: 5)
| WHERE avg_disk_queue_depth > 5.0
| SORT avg_disk_queue_depth DESC
